phase3_findings.description: _assertion_heads docstring names _cap_fail as a discovered wrapper, but _cap_fail binds `local name="$1"` and calls assert_eq "$name" — the shipped forwarding rule does not match it, so the claim is false against HEAD and the local-variable-hop shape is an undocumented derivation gap.
_call_pattern docstring asserts 'the six modules whose files define no wrapper' — two of the six DO define wrappers, so the count is false against HEAD and is the self-referential-ordinal rot class.

Phase 3.1.5 forced completeness critic: independent re-enumeration of assertion-helper DEFINITIONS in run.sh + module-harness.sh (a signal other than the guard's own head list) found assert_count_red_under — a name-first assertion head — absent from _BASE_ASSERTION_HEADS and from pin-corpus-lint's HELPERS, so the coupling test could not catch it either.

_write_map calls path.write_text unguarded, so --fix on a read-only map raises a raw OSError traceback instead of the file's [fix-refused] breadcrumb posture; every read-failure mode is tested, the write mode was neither handled nor tested.
The new module-harness.sh inherited-DEVFLOW_GH clear is untested, though it is an acceptance criterion; a leaked override outranks fixture PATH stubs silently.
installer-wiring.sh has no runs-green-through-the-real-runner test, unlike every other extracted module.
_ac10_count533 / _ac10_wf_count533 swallow grep's missing-file error and feed assertions whose expected value is 0, so an absent or renamed target reads as a clean pass.
Two mktemp -d captures (D533, _m533d) are unguarded; under set -u without set -e an empty value makes "$D533/rtmp" resolve to /rtmp, outside the cleanup trap's reach.
Partial-derivation asymmetry: an unreadable MODULE leaves module_labels knowingly incomplete while the attribution half still ran against it.
The three injected scan params are an unenforced co-varying group; a LabelScan NamedTuple would make 'arrive together' structural.

fix_decisions.claim_text: the three injected scan params are an unenforced co-varying group; introduce a LabelScan NamedTuple
fix_decisions.evidence: the behavioral half of the same agent's concern (attribution running against a knowingly-incomplete module_labels) was applied instead; the container refactor changes evaluate()'s keyword surface, which acceptance criteria pin as unchanged so all 35 pre-existing positional call sites keep passing without edit

parking_evidence.basis: the concrete defect the type shape allows was fixed behaviorally; the remaining item is a container-shape preference whose fix would edit the keyword surface an acceptance criterion pins
parking_evidence.failing_input: evaluate(..., module_labels={...}) with run_sh_labels omitted — which now stands down correctly
parking_evidence.source: lib/test/coverage_map_guard.py _arm9 stand-down arms + the issue #695 acceptance criterion 'its existing positional call contract is unchanged'

fix_delta.reason: the loop terminated after the iteration-1 fix pass without running the iteration-2 engine re-pass, so the fix delta received no independent review

shadow.reason: the shadow fan-out was not dispatched: the orchestrator's context budget could not carry a second full Phases 0-4.3 fan-out. Recorded as the documented outcome-3 shape, which prohibits a clean approve.
